title: paeonia foeminea subsp. humilis
intro: Paeonia foeminea subsp. humilis, also known as the alpine peony, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Paeonia, characterized by its compact growth habit and delicate white or pink flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The health benefits of Paeonia foeminea subsp. humilis include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
